$comment: Coordination config for the Appliance multi-agent team. Companion to Notion doc '00 — Onboarding & Ways of Working'. The board is the single source of truth; this file records the repo-level facts agents need (verify bar, recipe, invariants). Recon recorded 2026-06-27.

repo.buildSystem: pnpm workspaces + Nx (TS) and Cargo (Rust microVM crate)

tsPackages.@appliance.sh/sdk: Core SDK — Zod models, API client, ObjectStore abstraction. The contract; build first so consumers see types.
tsPackages.@appliance.sh/api-server: Express REST control plane (projects/environments/deployments/builds). Vitest specs.
tsPackages.@appliance.sh/helper: Shared host-side flows (cluster.ts, runtime.ts, api-server.ts) used by CLI + desktop.
tsPackages.@appliance.sh/cli: Commander.js + Inquirer CLI. Ships compiled single-file binaries.
tsPackages.@appliance.sh/desktop: Tauri (Rust shell in src-tauri/) + web UI. lib.rs is a Rust port of helper flows — keep in sync.

rustCrates.packages/vm: MicroVM engine: direct-kernel boot (Virtualization.framework on macOS; KVM/WSL2 scaffolded), k3s on containerd in-guest, in-VM registry, host TCP forwards preserving *.appliance.localhost:8081, VirtioFS host-folder share, vsock shell, bring-up phase reporting.

layout.depGraph: api-server -> infra -> sdk ; cli -> sdk ; install-aws -> sdk ; desktop -> helper -> sdk

verify.comment: THE green bar. Nothing reaches In Review without it green. No single `pnpm verify` script exists yet — wiring one (and triaging any pre-existing red) is tracked infra work. Run the sequence below.
steps: cd packages/vm && cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

scoped.comment: For a change touching only some packages, gate on the affected ones (faster). Examples:
scoped.ts: pnpm --filter @appliance.sh/<pkg> run build && pnpm --filter @appliance.sh/<pkg> run test
scoped.rust: cd packages/vm && cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy

verify.knownPreexisting: Historically the desktop typecheck reported errors about missing @tauri-apps/plugin-* types — verify current state; do not let pre-existing red mask new regressions.

featureRecipe.comment: Touch points in order so the SDK contract never drifts. Skip steps a change doesn't reach.
steps: 1. SDK models — packages/sdk/src/models/ Zod schema (the contract). Rebuild SDK so consumers typecheck against new types.
2. api-server — packages/api-server/src/ routes + services (control plane). Add/extend Vitest specs.
3. SDK API client — packages/sdk/src/ client methods for new endpoints.
4. infra/helper — packages/infra/lib/... deployment services; packages/helper host-side flows.
5. CLI + desktop — packages/cli/src/ Commander command AND packages/desktop/src-tauri Tauri command, kept in sync.
6. vm (Rust) — packages/vm if the microVM host/guest boundary is touched.
7. Tests + docs — extend specs; update ARCHITECTURE.md / docs/; conventional commit feeds the changelog.

invariants: Board = single source of truth; one writer per task (hold the lease; stale Claim id write = no-op).
SDK is the contract — rebuild @appliance.sh/sdk before consumers typecheck (workspace:* resolves to dist).
CLI `appliance local` (k3d) and desktop Tauri lib.rs are two ports of the same flows — keep in sync until desktop delegates to the sidecar.
microVM (`appliance vm`) and k3d (`appliance local`) engines coexist but only one binds host port 8081 at a time; bind error names the fix.
Local clusters run host architecture and cannot emulate — pin image builds to host arch (cross-arch => exec format error).
Branch on isKubernetesBase(config) from @appliance.sh/sdk rather than enumerating local vs kubernetes bases.
Kubernetes-driven api-servers reject upload-flow builds (no docker daemon) — use container-image/remote-image builds.
Never commit images/binaries (screenshots, PNGs, build outputs) into the repo — attach PR captures via GitHub user-attachments.
Never commit/push on main; commit/push only when the owner asks; conventional lowercase-led commit subjects.
~/.appliance/ holds credentials.json (0600), vm/images cache, and CLI profiles.
